question.text: Under Rule 8, Cash Transaction Reports, CBWTRs and NPO transaction reports must be furnished to FIU-IND:

answer.text: Monthly, by the 15th day of the succeeding month
Quarterly, by the 15th day of the month succeeding the quarter
Within 7 working days of the transaction

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Rule 8(1) requires the Principal Officer to furnish information on CTRs, series of connected cash transactions (Rule 3(B)), NPO transactions (Rule 3(BA)), forged currency transactions (Rule 3(C)), and CBWTRs (Rule 3(E)) every month to the Director by the 15th day of the succeeding month. This is a monthly reporting cycle.
**The Trap:** The quarterly deadline (15th of month succeeding the quarter) applies only to IPRs (immovable property reports under Rule 3(F)). The 7-working-day deadline applies only to STRs. Candidates must match the correct deadline with the correct report type.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Series XXIV tests the same monthly reporting cycle. The IFSCA exam tests all three reporting frequencies: monthly (CTR/CBWTR/NPO), quarterly (IPR), and event-driven (STR within 7 working days).
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Three reporting cycles to remember: Monthly by 15th (CTR, CBWTR, NPO, forged currency), Quarterly by 15th (IPR), and 7 working days (STR). For IFSC entities, monthly CBWTR reporting is the most frequent obligation.
